task: Record an audio clip using Audio Recorder app and save it.

steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the phone's home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Chrome, Phone, Messages, and a Google search widget. The "Audio Recorder" app is not visible on this main screen.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open the Audio Recorder app → | Step 2: Start recording an audio clip | Step 3: Stop and save the recording

[Decision]
The objective is to record an audio clip using the Audio Recorder app. Since I am on the home screen and the app is not immediately visible among the pinned icons, I need to launch it. I will use the system's open function to launch the "Audio Recorder" app directly.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The current screen displays a welcome page for the "Audio Recorder" app. It shows the app logo, a welcome message "Welcome to a simple and fast application", and a prominent yellow "Get started" button at the bottom of the screen. The main recording interface is not yet visible.

[Reflection]
The previous plan anticipated starting the recording immediately in Step 2, but the app has opened to a first-time welcome screen that requires user interaction to proceed before reaching the main interface.

[Replan]
Step 1: Open the Audio Recorder app ✓ | Step 2: Dismiss welcome screen by proceeding → | Step 3: Start recording an audio clip | Step 4: Stop and save the recording

[Decision]
Based on the current screen state and the updated plan, I need to get past this welcome screen to access the actual recording interface. The "Get started" button is the clear call-to-action to proceed. I will tap this button to move forward.

[Memory]
App launched successfully, currently navigating through initial welcome screens. Main recording interface is expected next.

[Observation]
The current screen displays a "Setup" page for the Audio Recorder app, showing various configuration options like Theme, Naming convention, Recording format (M4a selected), Sample rate (44.1kHz), Bitrate (128 kbps), and Channel count (Stereo). At the bottom, there are two yellow buttons: "Reset" and "Apply".

[Reflection]
The previous plan expected to reach the main recording interface to start recording in Step 3. However, instead of the main interface, the app has presented an initial "Setup" screen that requires confirming these settings first.

[Replan]
Step 1-2: Open and proceed past welcome ✓ | Step 3: Confirm initial setup settings → | Step 4: Start recording an audio clip | Step 5: Stop and save the recording

[Decision]
Based on the current screen state and the updated plan, I need to clear this setup screen to reach the main interface. The default settings (M4a, 44.1kHz, 128 kbps, Stereo) are already selected and recommended. To proceed, I will tap the "Apply" button at the bottom right to save these settings and move forward.

[Memory]
App launched successfully. Navigating through initial setup screens; default audio settings (M4a, 44.1kHz, 128 kbps, Stereo) are being applied. Main recording interface is expected next.

[Observation]
The current screen displays the main interface of the "Audio Recorder" app. The screen shows a waveform area (currently empty), a timer at "00:00", a default file name "Record-100", and technical details "0.09 Mb, M4a, 44.1kHz". At the bottom center, there is a large, prominent white circular button, which is the standard icon for starting a recording.

[Plan Update]
Step 1-3: Confirm initial setup settings ✓ | Step 4: Start recording an audio clip → | Step 5: Stop and save the recording

[Decision]
Based on the current plan to start recording an audio clip, and observing the main interface is now ready, I need to initiate the recording process. The large white circular button at the bottom center is the record button. I will tap this button to begin capturing audio.

[Memory]
Initial setup completed with default settings (M4a, 44.1kHz). Main interface reached. Ready to record; next step will be stopping and saving after recording starts. File name is currently "Record-100".

[Observation]
The screen shows the Audio Recorder app actively recording, with a timer at "00:04" and the text "Recording..." displayed prominently. The interface has changed from the record button to a pause button in the center and a stop button (a square icon) on the bottom right.

[Plan Update]
Step 1-4: Start recording an audio clip ✓ | Step 5: Stop and save the recording →

[Decision]
Based on the current plan to stop and save the recording, and observing the active recording state, I need to end the recording session. The square icon on the bottom right of the screen is the standard stop button. I will tap this button to stop the recording and save the audio clip.

[Memory]
Recording initiated and captured 4 seconds of audio (00:04). File details: 0.04 Mb, M4a, 44.1kHz. Ready to finalize save process.

[Observation]
The screen displays a "New name" dialog box prompting to save the recording. The default name "Record-101" is pre-filled in the text field. Below the text field, there are "Cancel" and "Save" buttons. The keyboard is also visible at the bottom of the screen.

[Plan Update]
Step 1-4: Start recording an audio clip ✓ | Step 5: Stop and save the recording ✓ | Step 6: Confirm saving the file →

[Decision]
The current plan step is to confirm saving the file. The interface has presented a dialog to save the newly recorded audio clip with the default name "Record-101". To complete the task of saving the audio clip, I need to tap the yellow "Save" button on the dialog.

[Memory]
Audio clip recorded (4 seconds). Saving process initiated with default name "Record-101". File details: M4a, 44.1kHz. Next step is to finalize saving.

[Observation]
The screen shows the main interface of the Audio Recorder app. The audio clip has been saved, as indicated by the playback interface now showing the file "Record-101" with a duration of 00:07, a size of 0.09 Mb, and format M4a, 44.1kHz. The play button is visible, and the recording controls have returned to the default state after stopping and saving.

[Plan Update]
Step 1-4: Start recording an audio clip ✓ | Step 5: Stop and save the recording ✓ | Step 6: Confirm saving the file ✓ | Step 7: Verify task completion →

[Decision]
The current plan step is to verify task completion. The observation confirms that an audio clip ("Record-101") has been successfully recorded and saved with all necessary details displayed on the screen. The user's instruction was to "Record an audio clip using Audio Recorder app and save it", which has now been fully achieved. Therefore, I will declare the task complete.

[Memory]
Audio clip successfully recorded and saved as "Record-101" (duration 00:07, 0.09 Mb, M4a, 44.1kHz). Task requirements fulfilled.
